nodes.context: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Tampa, Florida, involved in gambling, narcotics, and racketeering.
Syndicate led by Charlie Wall in Tampa, controlling illegal gambling and corrupting officials.
Italian Mafia group led by Ignacio Antinori, involved in drug trafficking and Bolita games in Tampa.

nodes.original_text_name: smaller independent Italian gang led by Santo Trafficante Sr.
nodes.context: Independent Italian gang operating Bolita games in Tampa, later merged with Antinori's organization.
New York City Mafia family led by Tommy Lucchese, partnered with Trafficante Jr. in narcotics.
Mafia family led by John LaRocca, jointly controlled Sans Souci casino in Havana with Trafficante.

nodes.original_text_name: Five Families of New York City
nodes.context: The five major Mafia families in New York City, allowed to operate in Florida after 1987.
The Mafia in Sicily, with which LoScalzo maintained ties.
New York Mafia family whose New Jersey faction worked with Trafficante Miami faction in money laundering.
Faction of the Trafficante family based in Miami, led by Steven Raffa, involved in money laundering.
One of the Five Families of New York City, allied with the Trafficante crime family.
Italian American crime syndicate based in Chicago, allied with the Trafficante crime family.
Italian American Mafia family based in Dallas, Texas, allied with the Trafficante crime family.
One of the Five Families of New York City, allied with the Trafficante crime family.
Italian American Mafia family based in New Orleans, allied with the Trafficante crime family.

edges.context: Trafficante family suspected Charlie Wall of shooting Santo Jr. and had him killed in 1955.
Wall and Antinori fought a decade-long war known as the 'Era of Blood' in the 1930s.
Antinori invited Trafficante Sr. into his organization and they expanded Bolita games together.
Trafficante Jr. maintained a narcotic trafficking network with Tommy Lucchese and met frequently.
Trafficante Jr. entered an agreement with John LaRocca to jointly control the Sans Souci casino in Havana.
After 1987, Florida was declared open territory for the Five Families to operate.
Boss LoScalzo maintained ties to the Sicilian Mafia.
Steven Raffa worked with Genovese family mobster John Mamone in a money laundering operation.
The Miami faction was a part of the Trafficante family, led by Steven Raffa under boss LoScalzo.
The Bonanno crime family is listed as an ally of the Trafficante crime family.
The Chicago Outfit is listed as an ally of the Trafficante crime family.
The Dallas crime family is listed as an ally of the Trafficante crime family.
The Gambino crime family is listed as an ally of the Trafficante crime family.
The Genovese crime family is listed as an ally of the Trafficante crime family.
The New Orleans crime family is listed as an ally of the Trafficante crime family.
